problem: A test bid was sent but never appeared in BuildVision.
cause: Forwarding rule not actually active, address not verified on the client side, or a filter too narrow to match the sender.
resolution: Confirm the forwarding rule shows active on the client side and that Bids@BuildVision.io was verified. Widen the filter to cover bid-related senders. Re-send a test bid and confirm it lands in the correct org.

cause: Both email forwarding and the inbox connector are enabled for the same mailbox.
resolution: Pick one ingestion path. Disable either the forwarding rule or the inbox connection so a bid is only captured once.

problem: CRM integration fails to authenticate or shows permission errors.
cause: Insufficient CRM permissions on the connecting account, or the wrong auth method for that CRM.
resolution: Re-run authorization with an account that has deals + contacts access. Confirm the auth method for that CRM (supported-CRM list is a known gap, B-8 — check with ENG). Do not test sync until auth succeeds with no errors.

problem: BuildVision can't retrieve a document behind a SharePoint / Google Drive login.
cause: No saved credential on file matches the protected link.
resolution: Add a saved credential under User Settings → Saved Credentials for that provider, using the user's own login. Credentials are per user — the person receiving the link must add it under their own account. Re-process the email once saved.

problem: A user says they never got their invitation / credential email.
cause: Bounce, stuck send, wrong email on the user record, or the credential-trigger step was missed.
resolution: Check the send queue for bounces or stuck sends. Verify the email on the user record, correct it, and re-trigger the invitation. (The exact internal trigger step is a documentation gap, B-5.)

problem: A user logs in but sees the wrong organization or can't see their office.
cause: User placed in the wrong hierarchy node, or multi-office access not enabled.
resolution: Re-check the user's placement against the intake structure and move them to the correct office/line card. For managers covering multiple offices, confirm they can switch via My Profile → Other Accounts.

relatedSteps.label: Place each user in the correct org node

problem: A child office appears at the top level instead of under the parent.
cause: The child org was created without nesting it under the parent (orphaned org).
resolution: Re-nest the child org under the correct parent so the hierarchy renders as intended. Re-run the CS verification gate on the full org tree before adding users or data.

problem: Files appear stuck in a processing state.
cause: Large files simply take longer to extract; there's no documented expected time so 'stuck' is ambiguous.
resolution: Confirm the file size is large and give processing time — BuildVision notifies on completion. If it clearly exceeds a reasonable window, escalate to ENG. (Expected processing time is a known gap, B-3.)

problem: Reps say the catalog (“Representation”) doesn't match their current line card.
cause: Catalog loaded from an out-of-date source, or items need add/remove/correction.
resolution: Log the specific add/remove/correct items during the rep walkthrough and route them to the CX Lead, who turns corrections around the same day.

problem: Integration setup is blocked with no client IT/ops contact.
cause: Phase 2 was started before securing the client's IT/ops contact.
resolution: Stop and return to Intake. Confirm the IT/ops contact and get their acknowledgement before attempting forwarding or CRM setup. Escalate to CS if the client is unresponsive.
